note: All 8 case(s) below were published directly to TestRail outside this repo's /fetch-section -> /migrate-section pipeline and were never tracked locally until this backfill (2026-07-23). Case IDs and creation dates indicate they predate this migration project. Content is taken verbatim (HTML converted to plain text only) -- no v2 style-rewrite, Plan Gates extraction check, or Suite 6 source_case_ids cross-reference has been done yet; grill_status is honestly marked not-tracked-by-repo rather than assumed confirmed. live TestRail section is named "Consent Trends" (missing "Card") vs the doc's "Consent Trends Card".

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Consent trends card shows the empty state when no consent has been logged
cases.preconditions: No visitor consent has been recorded for the connected website (consent logging produces no data). C134

steps.content: Locate the "Consent trends" card on the dashboard.
steps.expected: The "Consent trends" card should be displayed with the "Last 7 days" range indicator.

steps.content: Observe the body of the card.
steps.expected: An empty-state illustration should be displayed with the message "No visitor consent has been recorded yet" and a prompt to enable consent logging.

steps.content: Confirm the consent logging prompt contains an actionable link.
steps.expected: "consent logging" should be rendered as a clickable link within the empty-state message.

steps.content: Confirm the card footer.
steps.expected: A "View all data" link with arrow icon should be displayed at the bottom of the card.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the consent logging link in the Consent trends empty state opens Advanced settings
cases.preconditions: No visitor consent has been recorded, so the Consent trends card shows its empty state with the "consent logging" link. C134

steps.content: Locate the "Consent trends" card empty-state message.
steps.expected: The message "No visitor consent has been recorded yet" should be displayed with a "consent logging" link.

steps.content: Click the "consent logging" link.
steps.expected: The Advanced settings page should be displayed.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Consent trends card renders the donut chart when consent has been logged
cases.preconditions: At least one visitor consent has been recorded for the connected website within the last 7 days. C205

steps.content: Locate the "Consent trends" card on the dashboard.
steps.expected: The "Consent trends" card should be displayed with the "Last 7 days" range indicator.

steps.content: Observe the centre of the card.
steps.expected: A donut chart should be displayed with the total consent count and the label "Total Consents" shown in its centre.

steps.content: Observe the chart legend.
steps.expected: The legend should list "Accepted" (green), "Rejected" (red), and "Partially Accepted" (blue) markers.

steps.content: Confirm the card footer.
steps.expected: A "View all data" link with arrow icon should be displayed at the bottom of the card.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Consent trends donut chart reflects an Accepted consent
cases.preconditions: The connected website displays the CookieYes banner. No prior consent for the test visitor exists. C205

steps.content: On the connected website, click "Accept All" in the CookieYes banner.
steps.expected: The banner should collapse and the accept consent should be recorded.

steps.content: Log in to the CookieYes webapp and open the dashboard.
steps.expected: The dashboard should be displayed with the "Consent trends" card.

steps.content: Observe the donut chart in the Consent trends card.
steps.expected: The donut chart should include a green ring segment representing accepted consent.

steps.content: Hover over the green segment of the donut chart.
steps.expected: A tooltip should display the accepted-consent percentage and the accepted count ("Accepted: [count]").

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Consent trends donut chart reflects a Rejected consent
cases.preconditions: The connected website displays the CookieYes banner. No prior consent for the test visitor exists. C205

steps.content: On the connected website, click "Reject All" in the CookieYes banner.
steps.expected: The banner should collapse and the reject consent should be recorded.

steps.content: Log in to the CookieYes webapp and open the dashboard.
steps.expected: The dashboard should be displayed with the "Consent trends" card.

steps.content: Observe the donut chart in the Consent trends card.
steps.expected: The donut chart should include a red ring segment representing rejected consent.

steps.content: Hover over the red segment of the donut chart.
steps.expected: A tooltip should display the rejected-consent percentage and the rejected count ("Rejected: [count]").

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Consent trends donut chart reflects a Partially Accepted consent
cases.preconditions: The connected website displays the CookieYes banner. No prior consent for the test visitor exists. C205

steps.content: On the connected website, click "Customize" in the CookieYes banner.
steps.expected: The banner's second-layer category view with per-category toggles should be displayed, with all toggles off except Necessary.

steps.content: Enable the toggle for one non-Necessary cookie category.
steps.expected: The selected category toggle should switch to the on state.

steps.content: Click "Save my Preferences".
steps.expected: The banner should collapse and a partially-accepted consent should be recorded.

steps.content: Log in to the CookieYes webapp and open the dashboard.
steps.expected: The dashboard should be displayed with the "Consent trends" card.

steps.content: Observe the donut chart in the Consent trends card.
steps.expected: The donut chart should include a blue ring segment representing partially-accepted consent.

steps.content: Hover over the blue segment of the donut chart.
steps.expected: A tooltip should display the partially-accepted percentage and count ("Partially-accepted: [count]").

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Consent trends donut chart reflects Accepted, Rejected, and Partially Accepted consents together
cases.preconditions: The connected website displays the CookieYes banner and the Revisit Consent widget is enabled so consent can be re-submitted. Accepted, Rejected, and Partially-accepted consents have each been recorded for the website. C205

steps.content: Record an accepted consent (Accept All), a rejected consent (Revisit Consent then Reject All), and a partially-accepted consent (Revisit Consent, Customize, enable one non-Necessary category, Save my Preferences) on the connected website.
steps.expected: All three consent types should be recorded for the website.

steps.content: Log in to the CookieYes webapp and open the dashboard.
steps.expected: The dashboard should be displayed with the "Consent trends" card.

steps.content: Observe the donut chart in the Consent trends card.
steps.expected: The donut chart should display green, red, and blue ring segments simultaneously.

steps.content: Hover over the green segment.
steps.expected: A tooltip should display the accepted percentage and count.

steps.content: Hover over the red segment.
steps.expected: A tooltip should display the rejected percentage and count.

steps.content: Hover over the blue segment.
steps.expected: A tooltip should display the partially-accepted percentage and count.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the View all data link on the Consent trends card opens the reports page
cases.preconditions: At least one visitor consent has been recorded so the Consent trends card is populated. C205

steps.content: Locate the "View all data" link with arrow at the bottom of the Consent trends card.
steps.expected: The "View all data" link should be displayed at the bottom of the card.

steps.content: Click the "View all data" link.
steps.expected: The website reports page (/website-reports) should be displayed.
